ab: qazii ya simaq a saqazi mapariqaz ita?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: ua, a’intua ita amapariqaz?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: isui ita lhupilhupishan a taun mitalha? apashtiruq iamin.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: usui ita talhuqan mitalha maqitan, a’ifazin Thau a hulus iaku mutantu.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: mathuaw lamaqitan iamin a sa-ku-laa ya makabukay. ya makabukay unai ihu makilhim iakin.
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: ashaunatusiza puhubuqiza wa qali, mathuaw mindaaq!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: qazii ya Tungkariri Lus’an ihu munai!
ch: W: 那你祖靈祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Tungkariri Lus’an!

ab: ua! miku’utantu iaku!
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: minu ihu qa amushuqish kataunan?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: maqa amutaun iaku fuifuilh patashan.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: minu ihu qa antu itusi mawalhnaq a pruq fuifuilh patashan?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: maqa mangqtu iaku naak a kataunan a pruq, numa miku iaku lhungqizi kataunan a thau.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: mathuaw ma’ania ihu tunuun, tima fuilh ihun?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: naak a apuki’ay, lamathuaw thithu ma’ania tunuun maqusum a tanatuqash isa naam a kataunan.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: minu ihu qa amangqtu minfazaq tunuun sa maqusum?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: maqa tunuun sa maqusum mita wa kazakazash, mangqtu iaku izai a kazakazash ata pu’ukai.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: haya sunda wa qilha mathuaw maqitan ya qilhan.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: iaku min’ananak pinqilha!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: mathuaw ma’ania ihu, amindaaq ya kmalawa haya qilha?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: antu mindaaq, tata wa ripay painan aminqilhaiza. pintatan sunda wa zashuq masa kamar, numa pahiburan sa bulbul, numa ribuqin pisai qatapay, numa pusai sa tahamish.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米和小米先蒸熟，再加入麴粉一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
